wave: Wave 7: Review Hardening + Type Safety + Observability

items.name: Rename tool_block_stop → content_block_stop
validation: SdkContentBlockStopEvent type renamed, kind value is 'content_block_stop' ✓
App.tsx switch case updated to 'content_block_stop' ✓
session-worker.ts handles 'content_block_stop' (clears activeToolId) ✓
Test updated and passing: extractSdkEvent returns content_block_stop kind ✓

items.name: Document kind discriminant choice
validation: Comment block added explaining why 'kind' not 'type' — prevents future 'fix' attempts ✓

items.name: Fix tool_input_delta toolId extraction
validation: SdkToolInputDeltaEvent.toolId type changed to string | null ✓
extractSdkEvent returns null (not empty string) when contentBlock.id absent ✓
session-worker.ts tracks activeToolId from tool_use_start ✓
tool_input_delta uses activeToolId as fallback; drops frame if both null ✓
2 new tests: null toolId when absent, valid toolId when present ✓

items.name: Promote frameId to module scope
validation: frameId declared at module scope (line ~52), monotonically increasing across messages ✓
Comment explains why: per-message reset broke MessageBuffer.since() on reconnect ✓

items.name: Guard result send after error
validation: hadError flag set in catch block ✓
Result IPC skipped when hadError is true — early return after shutdown check ✓
No behavioral change (settled flag still protects parent), reduces wasteful IPC ✓

items.name: Single source of truth for WS client types
validation: Compile-time assertion: ZodOutput extends WsClientMessage extends ZodOutput ✓
Build fails if Zod schema and TypeScript union diverge ✓
Removed `as WsClientMessage` cast from validateWsMessage ✓
TypeScript compiles with zero errors ✓

items.name: Exhaustive switch enforcement
validation: session-worker.ts switch has default: { const _: never = event; } ✓
App.tsx switch has default: { const _: never = event; } ✓
session-worker.ts now handles content_block_stop, text_block_start, message_start (no-op) ✓
Adding a new SdkEvent kind without handling it causes compile error ✓

items.name: Extract ALLOWED_FRAME_TYPES to ipc-protocol.ts
validation: ALLOWED_FRAME_TYPES exported from ipc-protocol.ts at module scope ✓
serve.ts imports and uses the module-level constant (no per-call reconstruction) ✓
ReadonlySet<string> type allows runtime string checks ✓
New test verifies export and contents ✓

items.name: Eliminate residual as any cast in serve.ts
validation: messageBuffer.push uses `frame as unknown as WsToken | WsToolUseStart` (no `as any`) ✓
WsToken and WsToolUseStart imported from types/ws.ts ✓
Frame type narrowed by the ALLOWED_FRAME_TYPES check + type guard ✓

items.name: safeSend wrapper for ws.send
validation: safeSend(ws, data) helper: try/catch around ws.send(JSON.stringify(data)) ✓
Used in all post-processing ws.send calls in handleMessage ✓
Used in approval cleanup and error paths ✓
Used in worker frame relay, tool approval, result, status, budget frames ✓
Removed manual try/catch in approval rejection (safeSend handles it) ✓

items.name: Minimize worker config exposure
validation: WorkerConfig interface defined in ipc-protocol.ts with only needed fields ✓
toWorkerConfig() helper in serve.ts extracts model, effort, tools, hooks, serve.logging ✓
No serve.rateLimits, serve.privacy, serve.maxWorkers sent to worker ✓
session-worker.ts casts WorkerConfig to HarnessConfig (structural subset) ✓

items.name: WS schema tightening
validation: WsMessageSchema content field has .max(200_000) ✓
WsSubscribeSchema lastMessageId has .max(Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) ✓
4 new tests: content >200K rejected, 200K accepted, agentId >200 rejected, negative lastMessageId rejected ✓

items.name: safeCompare length-safety JSDoc
validation: JSDoc documents that timingSafeEqual is only constant-time for equal-length inputs ✓
Notes all current callers use fixed-length SHA-256 hex (64 chars) ✓
Warns about timing leak if used with variable-length inputs ✓
3 new tests: equal hex, different hex (same length), different length ✓

items.name: Bounded health arrays
validation: maybePrune() called on every recordError/recordSuccess ✓
Prunes entries older than 1 hour every 1000 insertions ✓
insertionCount resets after each prune cycle ✓
No memory growth under sustained load without deep health calls ✓
New test: 1001 insertions triggers prune without crash ✓

items.name: WorkerManager.getStats() method
validation: getStats() returns { active, max, utilization } ✓
Utilization rounded to 3 decimal places ✓
serve.ts health monitor lambda simplified to () => workerManager.getStats() ✓
New test: getStats returns correct WorkerPoolStats structure ✓

items.name: Wave 6 test coverage gaps
validation: maxWorkers edge values: 0 → default(20), -1 → 1, NaN → default(20), '5' → 5 ✓
WS schema: agentId >200 chars rejected, negative lastMessageId rejected ✓
WS schema: content >200K rejected, 200K accepted ✓
All 282 tests pass (93 in session-worker.test.ts) ✓

items.name: CLI credentials check command
validation: `credentials check --agent <name>` reports legacy vs strict mode ✓
Shows granted/missing keys per grant with tool domains ✓
Works with default agent if --agent not specified ✓

items.name: CLI access create command
validation: `access create --name <name> --agents <list>` generates token ✓
Appends entry to access.yaml (creates file if missing) ✓
Raw token shown once; only hash persisted ✓

items.name: CLI status command
validation: `status <agent>` reads runs.jsonl and shows last 10 runs ✓
Displays timestamp, exit code, duration, prompt snippet ✓
Gracefully handles missing runs file ✓

items.name: CLI preflight command
validation: `preflight --agent <name>` validates agent exists, IDENTITY.md parses ✓
Checks credentials grants for missing keys ✓
Checks sandbox/egress config ✓
Checks ANTHROPIC_API_KEY presence ✓
Exit code 0 on all-pass, 1 on any failure ✓

items.name: Token rotation mechanism
validation: `access rotate --name <name>` generates new token for existing user ✓
Updates token_hash in access.yaml ✓
Shows old hash prefix and new raw token ✓
Active sessions with old token rejected on next request (existing auth flow) ✓
